text:
World War II (1939–1945) was a conflict on a global scale, which involved or affected most of the world's countries, and it ended up being the deadliest war of mankind. It initially started in Europe and then spread to Asia, Africa, and the seas of the globe. In a way, the war was an outcome of the unresolved problems from World War I, the severe terms of the Versailles Treaty, the economic depression, and the rise of the fascist regimes in Nazi Germany, Fascist Italy, and Imperial Japan.

The aggressor action was the invasion of Poland by the Nazis on September 1, 1939, which was formally regarded as the beginning of the war. Adolf Hitler was at the helm of the Nazi Germany. Germany's enemies, i.e., Britain and France, responded to this act by declaring war against Germany. Germany was able to achieve a lot of her military objectives in the following two years through a violent and fast-moving attack method called 'Blitzkrieg' (lightning war). In this way, they managed to occupy western parts of Europe which include the countries referred to as the Low Countries (Belgium, the Netherlands and Luxembourg), and some areas in Scandinavian besides France, Italy became Germany's friend, and the Axis powers appeared to be invincible on the continent.

Japan had been busy in Asia for several years by the time she invaded Manchuria in 1931 and then China in 1937. Wanting also to secure sufficient resources, Japan aspired to be the ruler of the Pacific through the creation of an empire. On December 7, 1941, a surprise air attack at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii, carried out by Japan had the effect that the US entered the war on the side of the Allies. The Allies consisted of countries like Britain, the Soviet Union (which joined the war after Germany attacked it in 1941), China, and many other powers.

World War II was a war that was fought on various fronts. Europe saw the largest and the bloodiest battles such as those of Stalingrad and Kursk on the Eastern Front, which actually were turning points in the war against Germany. The Soviet Union was the main battlefield, and she suffered most of the deaths among the Allies. The drama in North Africa saw the Allies fighting against the Axis troops, and after a while, they stormed and took over Italy too. The Western Front was thus opened through the D-Day assault by the Allied troops on Normandy, France, on June 6, 1944, and they were able to make a pincer movement against Germany together with the Soviets who were attacking from the east.

The war that was full of terrors first and foremost towards non-combatants in the various lands under the Nazis' control. As the Nazis executed their horrible plan called the Holocaust, millions of people were also killed. The Jewish people who suffered most were six million of them. Other victims included Roma (Gypsies), handicapped people, political opponents, and prisoners of war. Camps like Auschwitz that were set up for storage, labor, and extermination became the symbols of the industrialized killing of humans and racial hatred.

Some U.S. and Allies initiations made a play of "island-hopping" in order to debate Japan on a string of key islands (territories). The capture of the last three islands made Japan a trapped country and showed how Japan would stoically fight to a point of death to avoid ultimate surrender. The United States did not hesitate to drop atomic bombs on two main Japanese cities- Hiroshima and Nagasaki in August 1945- to end the war quickly and to avoid the war on their home islands. Shortly after Tokyo announced its complete surrender.

Germany was forced to surrender in Europe (May 1945) and Hitler was no longer alive as he had taken his own life, and Berlin had already been occupied by Soviet forces. Thus, the war ended with victories of the Allies over both Germany and Japan.

The aftermath was as big as the war was. Death toll was in tens of millions, cities were in a state of wreck and ruin, and people numbering in millions were made homeless. The United Nations came into being as a means of avoiding wars on such a large scale in the future.
